deck.name: Sec 1 Essentials (test)
deck.description: Core vocabulary for Sec 1 students. 60 essential verbs, adjectives, adverbs, and nouns for everyday school and personal life.

cards.definition: To say what someone or something is like.

example_sentences.sentence: Can you describe what you saw at the scene?

cards.definition: To look at how two things are similar or different.

example_sentences.sentence: The teacher asked us to compare the two characters in the story.

cards.definition: To make something clear by giving details.

example_sentences.sentence: Please explain why you were late to class.

cards.definition: To give an idea for someone to think about.

example_sentences.sentence: My friend suggested that we study together before the exam.

cards.definition: To make something better, or to get better.

example_sentences.sentence: I practised every day to improve my writing skills.

cards.definition: To choose something after thinking about it.

example_sentences.sentence: I couldn't decide which CCA to join.

cards.definition: To suddenly understand something.

example_sentences.sentence: I only realized my mistake after I handed in the paper.

cards.definition: To see or become aware of something.

example_sentences.sentence: She noticed that her friend seemed upset during lunch.

cards.definition: To get ready for something.

example_sentences.sentence: I stayed up late to prepare for the science test.

cards.definition: To form a picture of something in your mind.

example_sentences.sentence: Try to imagine how the character felt at that moment.

cards.definition: To find something for the first time.

example_sentences.sentence: We discovered a shortcut through the park on the way home.

cards.definition: To give someone confidence to do something.

example_sentences.sentence: My teacher encouraged me to enter the writing competition.

cards.definition: To stay away from something.

example_sentences.sentence: She tried to avoid the topic whenever her parents asked about her grades.

cards.definition: To succeed in doing something after effort.

example_sentences.sentence: He worked hard to achieve his goal of getting into the school team.

cards.definition: To show your thoughts or feelings in words.

example_sentences.sentence: Writing in a journal helped her express how she felt.

cards.definition: To say that you are unhappy about something.

example_sentences.sentence: He complained that the homework was too difficult.

cards.definition: To say sorry for something you did.

example_sentences.sentence: She apologized to her friend after saying something hurtful.

cards.definition: To tell someone that something is good.

example_sentences.sentence: Can you recommend a good book for our English project?

cards.definition: To succeed in doing something difficult.

example_sentences.sentence: I finally managed to finish all my assignments before dinner.

cards.definition: To have an effect on what someone does or thinks.

example_sentences.sentence: Social media can influence the way teenagers think about themselves.

cards.definition: Wanting to know or learn about something.

example_sentences.sentence: I was curious about how the experiment would turn out.

cards.definition: Feeling sure about yourself and your abilities.

example_sentences.sentence: She felt confident after practising her speech many times.

cards.definition: Feeling worried or afraid about something.

example_sentences.sentence: I felt nervous before giving my presentation to the class.

cards.definition: Able to wait calmly without getting annoyed.

example_sentences.sentence: You need to be patient when learning a new skill.

cards.definition: Telling the truth and not cheating.

example_sentences.sentence: It is important to be honest even when the truth is difficult.

cards.definition: Happy to give time, money, or help to others.

example_sentences.sentence: My classmate was generous enough to share her notes with me.

cards.definition: Able to be trusted to do what is expected.

example_sentences.sentence: A reliable friend shows up when you need them most.

cards.definition: Showing strong excitement about something.

example_sentences.sentence: She was enthusiastic about joining the debate team.

cards.definition: Having the duty to take care of something.

example_sentences.sentence: As the class monitor, he was responsible for taking attendance.

cards.definition: Able to do things by yourself without help.

example_sentences.sentence: Secondary school teaches you to be more independent with your studies.

cards.definition: Good at making new ideas or things.

example_sentences.sentence: Our art teacher is very creative with her lesson ideas.

cards.definition: Able to make good decisions.

example_sentences.sentence: It was sensible of her to bring an umbrella when the sky looked cloudy.

cards.definition: Having a strong wish to succeed.

example_sentences.sentence: He is ambitious and already knows which university he wants to attend.

cards.definition: Feeling worried about something that may happen.

example_sentences.sentence: I felt anxious waiting for my exam results.

cards.definition: Feeling thankful for something.

example_sentences.sentence: I was grateful when my teacher gave me extra time to finish the test.

cards.definition: Well known to you because you have seen it before.

example_sentences.sentence: The tune sounded familiar, but I couldn't remember the name of the song.

cards.definition: Easy to see or understand.

example_sentences.sentence: It was obvious from her smile that she was happy with the result.

cards.definition: Honest and saying what you really feel.

example_sentences.sentence: His apology seemed sincere, so she decided to forgive him.

cards.definition: So good that people admire it.

example_sentences.sentence: Her science project was impressive and won first place.

cards.definition: Very important and necessary.

example_sentences.sentence: Good sleep is essential for doing well in school.

cards.definition: In a way that gives attention to detail.

example_sentences.sentence: Read the question carefully before you answer.

cards.definition: Quickly and without warning.

example_sentences.sentence: It suddenly started to rain while we were walking home.

cards.definition: Happening often.

example_sentences.sentence: She frequently forgets to bring her water bottle to school.

cards.definition: Slowly, over a period of time.

example_sentences.sentence: My confidence in speaking English gradually improved over the term.

cards.definition: In a way that is easy to see or understand.

example_sentences.sentence: Please speak clearly so everyone in the room can hear you.

cards.definition: In the end, after a long time.

example_sentences.sentence: I kept trying and eventually got the answer right.

cards.definition: Almost never.

example_sentences.sentence: He rarely misses school, even when he feels unwell.

cards.definition: In a way that is easy to see.

example_sentences.sentence: She was obviously tired after the long training session.

cards.definition: Right away, without delay.

example_sentences.sentence: She immediately put up her hand when she knew the answer.

cards.definition: In a way that shows you are sure of yourself.

example_sentences.sentence: He walked up to the stage and spoke confidently.

cards.definition: In a truthful way.

example_sentences.sentence: She honestly told her friend that the outfit didn't suit her.

cards.definition: In a way that is not joking.

example_sentences.sentence: You should take your health seriously and not skip meals.

cards.definition: The energy you use to do something.

example_sentences.sentence: With a lot of effort, she managed to pass the difficult exam.

cards.definition: A chance to do something good.

example_sentences.sentence: Joining the student council was a great opportunity to develop leadership skills.

cards.definition: Something difficult that tests your ability.

example_sentences.sentence: Moving to a new school was a big challenge at first.

cards.definition: The process of getting better or moving forward.

example_sentences.sentence: My teacher said I had made good progress in my writing this term.

cards.definition: An idea about what someone should do.

example_sentences.sentence: She gave me useful advice on how to manage my time better.

cards.definition: Something good you succeed in doing.

example_sentences.sentence: Winning the spelling bee was her greatest achievement that year.

cards.definition: A choice you make after thinking.

example_sentences.sentence: Choosing which subject to take was a difficult decision.

cards.definition: Something done wrong by accident.

example_sentences.sentence: Making a mistake is okay as long as you learn from it.
